title: Resinous Turkish Turkish Delight Pastry
description: A traditional Turkish pastry featuring a resinous, glassy-jelly filling and a flaky lamination-puff crust, finished with a five spice powder dusting.
instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, graham flour, and five spice powder.
Add the cold unsalted butter to the dry ingredients and use a pastry blender to work it into the mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the granulated sugar, egg yolks, water, and lemon juice.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and roll it out to a thickness of 1/4 inch (6 mm).
Transfer the dough to the prepared baking sheet and use a pastry brush to apply the melted unsalted butter.
Sprinkle the confectioners' sugar evenly over the dough, leaving a 1 inch (2.5 cm) border around the edges.
Bake the pastry for 25-30 minutes, or until it is golden brown and puffed.
Remove the pastry from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Transfer the pastry to a wire rack and let it cool completely.
Once the pastry is cool, use a sharp knife to cut it into diamond-shaped pieces.
Dust the pastry with confectioners' sugar and serve immediately.
